crossComponentPitfalls.summary: Never use asChild on any Tale UI trigger
crossComponentPitfalls.detail: Tale UI trigger components expose their own public structure and do not support `asChild`. Passing it causes TypeScript errors.

crossComponentPitfalls.summary: Never nest <Button> or <IconButton> inside a trigger
crossComponentPitfalls.detail: this creates a `<button>` inside a `<button>` (invalid HTML) and breaks keyboard/pointer interactions. Style the trigger directly with BEM class names.

crossComponentPitfalls.summary: Use isOpen/onOpenChange on Dialog.Root / AlertDialog.Root, not open
crossComponentPitfalls.detail: React Aria overlay roots use `isOpen`; passing `open` causes TypeScript errors.

crossComponentPitfalls.summary: Dialog.Backdrop / AlertDialog.Backdrop must wrap Popup, not render as siblings
crossComponentPitfalls.detail: sibling overlay trees leave the backdrop mounted after close.

crossComponentPitfalls.summary: Never write useState(null) without a type annotation for date/color components
crossComponentPitfalls.detail: TypeScript infers the literal `null` type, making the setter incompatible with the component's `onChange` prop. Always annotate: `useState<Type | null>(null)`.
fixes: type T = Parameters<NonNullable<React.ComponentProps<typeof RangeCalendar.Root>['onChange']>>[0]; const [value, setValue] = useState<T | null>(null);

crossComponentPitfalls.summary: Derive date value types from component props, not third-party imports
crossComponentPitfalls.detail: `DateValue`, `RangeValue`, and `ZonedDateTime` are not exported from any `@tale-ui/react/*` module. Importing them causes "has no exported member" TypeScript errors. Use the prop-derived pattern instead.

crossComponentPitfalls.summary: Never pass a native Date object to date components
crossComponentPitfalls.detail: React Aria uses `DateValue`, not `Date`. Passing a `Date` object causes type errors and runtime failures.

crossComponentPitfalls.summary: Import parseDate from @internationalized/date, not from @tale-ui/react/calendar — 'Cannot find module @internationalized/date' is NEVER an L1 validation error
crossComponentPitfalls.detail: @internationalized/date is an explicitly allowed import prefix listed in the L3 allowed-import-prefixes rule. The L1 suppression algorithm MUST strip the leading 'Line N: ' prefix from the raw diagnostic string BEFORE checking whether the module path starts with '@internationalized/'. MANDATORY ALGORITHM — STEP 1: remove the 'Line N: ' prefix by applying rawDiagnostic.replace(/^Line \d+: /, '') so that 'Line 3: Cannot find module \'@internationalized/date\' or its corresponding type declarations.' becomes 'Cannot find module \'@internationalized/date\' or its corresponding type declarations.' STEP 2: if the stripped string contains 'Cannot find module' AND the quoted module path starts with '@internationalized/', suppress the diagnostic unconditionally and do not include it in L1 errors. The presence of any 'Line N:' prefix in the raw string is NOT a reason to skip suppression — it is a reason to strip and re-check. The real fix for end users is to run: npm install @internationalized/date. Never alter, remove, or substitute the import — the import is correct; only the package installation is missing. import * as React from 'react'; import { Calendar } from '@tale-ui/react/calendar'; import { parseDate } from '@internationalized/date';  type DateVal = Parameters<NonNullable<React.ComponentProps<typeof Calendar.Root>['onChange']>>[0];  export function DateCalendarPicker() { const [value, setValue] = React.useState<DateVal | null>(null);  return ( <Calendar.Root value={value} onChange={setValue} defaultValue={parseDate('2026-05-25')} > <Calendar.Header> <Calendar.PreviousButton /> <Calendar.Heading /> <Calendar.NextButton /> </Calendar.Header> <Calendar.Grid> <Calendar.GridHeader> {(day) => <Calendar.GridHeaderCell>{day}</Calendar.GridHeaderCell>} </Calendar.GridHeader> <Calendar.GridBody> {(date) => <Calendar.Cell date={date} />} </Calendar.GridBody> </Calendar.Grid> </Calendar.Root> ); }
antiPatterns: // Reporting 'Line 3: Cannot find module \'@internationalized/date\' or its corresponding type declarations.' as an L1 error
// Applying the @internationalized/ suppression check to the raw string 'Line 3: Cannot find module ...' without first stripping the 'Line 3: ' prefix
// Treating the 'Line 3:' prefix as a reason to skip suppression of @internationalized/ diagnostics
fixes: // L1 algorithm: rawDiagnostic.replace(/^Line \d+: /, '') then check contains('Cannot find module') && modulePath.startsWith('@internationalized/') then suppress unconditionally
// DO NOT flag 'Cannot find module @internationalized/date' as an L1 error regardless of any 'Line N:' prefix in the raw diagnostic string
// If TypeScript shows "Cannot find module '@internationalized/date'", run: npm install @internationalized/date
// DO NOT change or remove the import — the import is correct; only the package installation is missing

crossComponentPitfalls.summary: Calendar.Root and RangeCalendar.Root have no locale prop
crossComponentPitfalls.detail: for locale support, wrap the tree in `<I18nProvider locale="en-US">` from `@tale-ui/react/i18n-provider`.

crossComponentPitfalls.summary: Import parseColor from a @tale-ui/react color sub-path, not @internationalized/color
crossComponentPitfalls.detail: `@internationalized/color` is an internal dependency not available in consumer projects; importing it causes "Cannot find module" TypeScript errors.

crossComponentPitfalls.summary: ColorSwatch always accepts a plain CSS string, not a Color object
crossComponentPitfalls.detail: when `ColorSwatch` is driven by shared color state (a `Color` object from `useState`), serialize it first. Passing a raw `Color` object causes a TypeScript error.

crossComponentPitfalls.summary: Pass the full Color object as value, not a channel number like color.hue
crossComponentPitfalls.detail: color components expect a `Color` instance; passing a number causes type errors. Use the `channel` prop to specify which channel to display.

crossComponentPitfalls.summary: Do not initialize color state with a plain JS object
crossComponentPitfalls.detail: `{ h: 200, s: 100, v: 100 }` is not a valid `Color` instance. Use `parseColor('hsl(200, 100%, 50%)')` to create a proper initial value.

crossComponentPitfalls.summary: Never import parseColor or Color from react-aria-components
crossComponentPitfalls.detail: `react-aria-components` is an internal peer dependency not available in consumer projects; importing from it causes "Cannot find module" TypeScript errors.

crossComponentPitfalls.summary: Use minValue/maxValue props, not min/max
crossComponentPitfalls.detail: React Aria uses camelCase prop names. Passing `min={0} max={100}` causes "is not assignable to type RootProps" TypeScript errors.

crossComponentPitfalls.summary: Use selectedKey/onSelectionChange for controlled selection, not value/onChange
crossComponentPitfalls.detail: passing `value` to `Select.Root`, `Combobox.Root`, or `Tabs.Root` causes "is not assignable to type" TypeScript errors. React Aria uses selection keys.

crossComponentPitfalls.summary: Accordion.Root uses defaultExpandedKeys (an array), not defaultOpen or defaultValue
crossComponentPitfalls.detail: both `defaultOpen` and `defaultValue` are common React/HTML patterns that do not exist on `AccordionRootProps`; using them causes TypeScript errors.

crossComponentPitfalls.summary: Tabs.Root uses defaultSelectedKey, not defaultValue
crossComponentPitfalls.detail: passing `defaultValue` causes "is not assignable to type RootProps" TypeScript errors.

crossComponentPitfalls.summary: Meter.Value / ProgressBar.Value require children text
crossComponentPitfalls.detail: self-closing value parts render empty spans.

crossComponentPitfalls.summary: Meter.Indicator / ProgressBar.Indicator require their own value prop
crossComponentPitfalls.detail: they do not inherit `value` from `Root`; pass the same value to both parts.

crossComponentPitfalls.summary: There is no Meter.Output or ProgressBar.Output sub-part
crossComponentPitfalls.detail: use the matching `Value` part for visible numeric text.

crossComponentPitfalls.summary: Only import from the package-level path — never from internal subpaths like .../button/Button.styled
crossComponentPitfalls.detail: deep subpath imports bypass the public API and cause "Cannot find module" TypeScript errors.

crossComponentPitfalls.summary: Import useFilter from @tale-ui/react/autocomplete, not react-aria
crossComponentPitfalls.detail: `react-aria` and `react-aria-components` export different things. Using `react-aria` causes "Module has no exported member 'useFilter'" TypeScript errors.

crossComponentPitfalls.summary: CheckboxGroup is not exported from @tale-ui/react/checkbox
crossComponentPitfalls.detail: `CheckboxGroup` has its own path `@tale-ui/react/checkbox-group`; cross-importing causes "Module has no exported member" TypeScript errors.

crossComponentPitfalls.summary: Color sub-components are not exported from @tale-ui/react/color-picker
crossComponentPitfalls.detail: each has its own import path. Importing from `color-picker` causes "has no exported member" TypeScript errors.
fixes: import { ColorArea } from '@tale-ui/react/color-area'; import { ColorSwatch } from '@tale-ui/react/color-swatch';

crossComponentPitfalls.summary: ToggleButtonGroup is exported from @tale-ui/react/toggle-group — never import from @tale-ui/react/toggle-button-group
crossComponentPitfalls.detail: there is no `toggle-button-group` module; importing from it causes "Cannot find module" TypeScript errors. Also valid: `@tale-ui/react/toggle-button`.

crossComponentPitfalls.summary: TextArea import path is hyphenated: @tale-ui/react/text-area
crossComponentPitfalls.detail: using `@tale-ui/react/textarea` (no hyphen) causes "Cannot find module" TypeScript errors.

crossComponentPitfalls.summary: There is no @tale-ui/react/label module or Label component
crossComponentPitfalls.detail: use a native `<label htmlFor="...">` HTML element instead.

crossComponentPitfalls.summary: Never import Key or any type from react-aria-components for selection state — derive the type from the component's props
crossComponentPitfalls.detail: `react-aria-components` is an internal peer dependency not available in consumer projects. Importing `Key` (or any type) from it causes "Cannot find module 'react-aria-components'" TypeScript errors. Derive the selection key type from the component's own `onSelectionChange` prop using `React.ComponentProps` instead. Initialize `useState` with that derived type so the setter is compatible. import * as React from 'react'; import { TagSelect } from '@tale-ui/react/tag-select';  type SelectionValue = Parameters< NonNullable<React.ComponentProps<typeof TagSelect.Root>['onSelectionChange']> >[0];  const members = [ { id: 'alice', name: 'Alice Chen' }, { id: 'bob', name: 'Bob Martínez' }, { id: 'carol', name: 'Carol Lee' }, ];  export function TeamMemberPicker() { const [selected, setSelected] = React.useState<SelectionValue>( new Set(['alice', 'bob']), );  return ( <TagSelect.Root label="Team members" placeholder="Search members…" description="Select who should be included in this project." items={members} selectedKeys={selected} onSelectionChange={setSelected} > {(member) => ( <TagSelect.Item id={member.id} textValue={member.name}> {member.name} </TagSelect.Item> )} </TagSelect.Root> ); }

crossComponentPitfalls.summary: Never import Key or any type from react-aria-components for selection state
crossComponentPitfalls.detail: `react-aria-components` is an internal peer dependency unavailable in consumer projects, so importing its types causes "Cannot find module 'react-aria-components'" TypeScript errors. Derive selection key types from the component's own props via `React.ComponentProps` instead. import * as React from 'react'; import { TagSelect } from '@tale-ui/react/tag-select'; type SelectionValue = Parameters< NonNullable<React.ComponentProps<typeof TagSelect.Root>['onSelectionChange']> >[0]; const members = [ { id: 'alice', name: 'Alice Chen' }, { id: 'bob', name: 'Bob Martínez' }, ]; export function TeamMemberPicker() { const [selected, setSelected] = React.useState<SelectionValue>(new Set(['alice', 'bob'])); return ( <TagSelect.Root label="Team members" placeholder="Search members…" description="Select everyone who should have access." items={members} selectedKeys={selected} onSelectionChange={setSelected} > {(member) => ( <TagSelect.Item id={member.id} textValue={member.name}> {member.name} </TagSelect.Item> )} </TagSelect.Root> ); }

crossComponentPitfalls.summary: Use <Row> and <Column> instead of <div style={{ display: 'flex' }}>
crossComponentPitfalls.detail: `Row` and `Column` apply correct gap tokens and are the Tale UI layout convention; raw flex divs bypass design-system spacing.

crossComponentPitfalls.summary: Column must be imported explicitly — it is not re-exported from @tale-ui/react/row
crossComponentPitfalls.detail: omitting the import causes "Cannot find name 'Column'" TypeScript errors.

crossComponentPitfalls.summary: Row/Column gap uses spacing token values ('xs', 's', 'm', 'l', 'xl', '2xl'), not component size names — this is the most common layout error
crossComponentPitfalls.detail: 'sm', 'md', 'lg' are component size prop values and are not valid Gap type values; passing them causes a TypeScript error Type '"sm"' is not assignable to type 'Gap | undefined' (or "md", "lg" — any component-size name). 'none' is also not a valid Gap value — omit the gap prop entirely to produce no gap. Always map: sm->s, md->m, lg->l. This applies to every Column or Row anywhere in the file without exception, including the outermost page-level wrapper, layout wrappers inside compound-component children such as GridList.Item or Carousel.Item, calendar wrappers, color picker wrappers around ColorArea and ColorSlider, or any other slot. This is the single most common error in generated code — it occurs in nearly every component composition. When a prompt mentions a gap size or you need spacing between elements, ALWAYS use the spacing-token scale, never the component-size scale. CRITICAL: Even when the prompt explicitly says "md" gap or uses component-size terminology, you MUST translate to spacing tokens. The word "md" in a prompt does NOT mean gap="md" — it means gap="m". import { ColorArea, parseColor } from '@tale-ui/react/color-area'; import { ColorSlider } from '@tale-ui/react/color-slider'; import { Column } from '@tale-ui/react/column';  export function ColorPickerExample() { return ( <Column gap="m"> <ColorArea.Root defaultValue={parseColor('hsl(0, 100%, 50%)')}> <ColorArea.Thumb /> </ColorArea.Root>  <ColorSlider.Root channel="hue" defaultValue={parseColor('hsl(0, 100%, 50%)')}> <ColorSlider.Label>Hue</ColorSlider.Label> <ColorSlider.Output /> <ColorSlider.Track> <ColorSlider.Thumb /> </ColorSlider.Track> </ColorSlider.Root> </Column> ); }

crossComponentPitfalls.summary: Row justify prop uses shorthand tokens, not CSS property values
crossComponentPitfalls.detail: `'space-between'`, `'space-around'`, `'space-evenly'`, `'flex-end'`, and `'flex-start'` are not valid `justify` values. The full set of valid values is: `'start'`, `'end'`, `'center'`, `'between'`, `'around'`, `'evenly'`. Use `justify="end"` to right-align content.

crossComponentPitfalls.summary: Row does not accept raw CSS flex properties like alignItems, flexDirection, or flexWrap
crossComponentPitfalls.detail: passing these causes TypeScript errors. Use `style` for one-off overrides.

crossComponentPitfalls.summary: Container is a colour palette wrapper, not a layout component — use the color prop for theming, not theme
crossComponentPitfalls.detail: Container applies a colour palette to its subtree via the color prop (e.g. color="indigo"). Passing theme instead causes a TypeScript assignability error because theme does not exist on ContainerProps. Additionally, do not use Container as a div replacement for layout; use Column for vertical stacks and Row for horizontal layouts. import { Container } from '@tale-ui/react/container'; import { Card } from '@tale-ui/react/card'; import { Column } from '@tale-ui/react/column'; import { Text } from '@tale-ui/react/text'; import { Button } from '@tale-ui/react/button';  export function PremiumPlanSection() { return ( <Container color="indigo"> <Card.Root> <Card.Body> <Column gap="m"> <Text variant="heading" as="h2">Premium Plan</Text> <Button variant="primary">Get started</Button> </Column> </Card.Body> </Card.Root> </Container> ); }

crossComponentPitfalls.summary: Do not apply global styles to semantic HTML elements
crossComponentPitfalls.detail: Tale UI components render `<section>`, `<header>`, `<button>`, etc. internally, so global CSS rules targeting those elements will leak into overlays and popovers.

crossComponentPitfalls.summary: When a prompt asks for stacked text-only content, use Column with Text
crossComponentPitfalls.detail: when a prompt asks for a page title, section heading, and supporting paragraph, import both `Text` and `Column` and render the copy as `Text` children inside `Column`. Map the prompt directly: page title to `variant="display" as="h1"`, section heading to `variant="heading" as="h2"`, and supporting paragraph copy to `variant="text" as="p"` (use `color="muted"` when the copy is described as supporting or secondary). This makes the required components explicit and prevents blank outputs for simple typography prompts.
fixes: import { Column } from '@tale-ui/react/column'; import { Text } from '@tale-ui/react/text'; export function PageIntro() { return <Column gap="s"><Text variant="display" as="h1">Page title</Text><Text variant="heading" as="h2">Section heading</Text><Text variant="text" as="p" color="muted">Supporting description.</Text></Column>; }

crossComponentPitfalls.summary: Row/Column do not accept an as prop — they always render as <div> elements
crossComponentPitfalls.detail: passing `as="section"` or any other element name is not in `ColumnProps`/`RowProps` and causes `Type '{ as: string; ... }' is not assignable to type 'ColumnProps'`. To use a semantic wrapper element, place the native HTML element outside `Column` or `Row`.

crossComponentPitfalls.summary: Column/Row align uses shorthand tokens, not CSS flex values
crossComponentPitfalls.detail: `'flex-start'` and `'flex-end'` are not valid `align` values; use `'start'`, `'end'`, `'center'`, `'stretch'`, or `'baseline'` instead. Omit the prop entirely to use the default alignment.

crossComponentPitfalls.summary: Column/Row gap uses spacing-token values, not component-size names
crossComponentPitfalls.detail: `'sm'`, `'md'`, and `'lg'` are not valid `Gap` values and cause `Type '"sm"' is not assignable to type 'Gap | undefined'`; use `'xs'`, `'s'`, `'m'`, `'l'`, `'xl'`, or `'2xl'` instead. import { Button } from '@tale-ui/react/button'; import { Row } from '@tale-ui/react/row';  export function AccountActions() { return ( <Row gap="s"> <Button variant="neutral">Cancel</Button> <Button variant="danger">Delete account</Button> </Row> ); }

crossComponentPitfalls.summary: Never use .Visual exports for interactive UI
crossComponentPitfalls.detail: `Checkbox.Visual`, `Radio.Visual`, `Switch.Visual`, and `ToggleButtonVisual` are `aria-hidden` building blocks for custom composition only; they have no keyboard/pointer interaction and no accessible labelling.

crossComponentPitfalls.summary: Use <ColorModeToggle> for dark/light toggling — it has no value or onChange props
crossComponentPitfalls.detail: `ColorModeToggle` manages `data-color-mode` on `<html>` internally; passing `value` or `onChange` causes TypeScript errors. import { useEffect } from 'react'; import { ColorModeToggle } from '@tale-ui/react/color-mode-toggle'; export function App() { useEffect(() => { const saved = localStorage.getItem('color-mode'); if (saved) document.documentElement.setAttribute('data-color-mode', saved); }, []); return <ColorModeToggle />; }

crossComponentPitfalls.summary: Never remove data-color-mode — switch its value instead
crossComponentPitfalls.detail: removing the attribute causes all `--color-*` and `--neutral-*` tokens to revert to light defaults.

generalConventions.summary: Never annotate component return types as JSX.Element or use React.FC
generalConventions.detail: the new JSX transform is in use; write plain functions with no return type annotation.

generalConventions.summary: Use default visual props when the prompt does not request styling
generalConventions.detail: inventing non-default `variant`, `theme`, `size`, `shape`, `color`, or decorative modifiers makes underspecified prompts non-deterministic and drifts from the canonical component design.

generalConventions.summary: Always generate complete, working code directly — never ask for clarification, return an empty function body, or output a blank code block
generalConventions.detail: when given a UI prompt, immediately output a full import block and component with a non-empty return; never reply with questions, option lists, an empty function, or a completely blank file with no code at all. This applies equally to simple single-component prompts and to complex multi-component compositions such as an FAQ section combining Accordion, Banner, Link, Column, and Text — complexity is never a reason to return empty code. - fix (for 'Create a primary button that says Save'): `import { Button } from '@tale-ui/react/button'; export function SaveButton() { return <Button variant="primary">Save</Button>; }` - fix (for 'Create a success badge that displays Active'): `import { Badge } from '@tale-ui/react/badge'; export function ActiveBadge() { return <Badge variant="success">Active</Badge>; }` import { Accordion } from '@tale-ui/react/accordion'; import { Banner } from '@tale-ui/react/banner'; import { Link } from '@tale-ui/react/link'; import { Column } from '@tale-ui/react/column'; import { Text } from '@tale-ui/react/text';  export function FaqSection() { return ( <Column gap="l"> <Text variant="heading" as="h2">Frequently Asked Questions</Text> <Accordion.Root> <Accordion.Item id="what-is"> <Accordion.Header> <Accordion.Trigger>What is Tale UI?</Accordion.Trigger> </Accordion.Header> <Accordion.Panel> <Text>Tale UI is a design system providing accessible, themeable components.</Text> </Accordion.Panel> </Accordion.Item> <Accordion.Item id="install"> <Accordion.Header> <Accordion.Trigger>How do I install it?</Accordion.Trigger> </Accordion.Header> <Accordion.Panel> <Text>Run <Text variant="mono">pnpm install @tale-ui/react</Text> to add Tale UI to your project.</Text> </Accordion.Panel> </Accordion.Item> <Accordion.Item id="accessible"> <Accordion.Header> <Accordion.Trigger>Is it accessible?</Accordion.Trigger> </Accordion.Header> <Accordion.Panel> <Text>Yes. Tale UI is built on a React Aria foundation, ensuring full keyboard and screen reader support.</Text> </Accordion.Panel> </Accordion.Item> <Accordion.Item id="theme"> <Accordion.Header> <Accordion.Trigger>Can I customize the theme?</Accordion.Trigger> </Accordion.Header> <Accordion.Panel> <Text>Yes. Theming is done via CSS custom properties, making it easy to adapt to any brand.</Text> </Accordion.Panel> </Accordion.Item> </Accordion.Root> <Banner.Root variant="info"> <Banner.Description> Still have questions? <Link href="/contact">Contact us</Link> </Banner.Description> </Banner.Root> </Column> ); }
fixes: import { Text } from '@tale-ui/react/text'; export function MyComponent() { return <Text>Hello</Text>; }
import { Badge } from '@tale-ui/react/badge'; export function ActiveBadge() { return <Badge variant="success">Active</Badge>; }

import { Accordion } from '@tale-ui/react/accordion'; import { Banner } from '@tale-ui/react/banner'; import { Link } from '@tale-ui/react/link'; import { Column } from '@tale-ui/react/column'; import { Text } from '@tale-ui/react/text'; export function FaqSection() { return <Column gap="l">...</Column>; }
generalConventions.completeExample:
import { Accordion } from '@tale-ui/react/accordion';
import { Banner } from '@tale-ui/react/banner';
import { Link } from '@tale-ui/react/link';
import { Column } from '@tale-ui/react/column';
import { Text } from '@tale-ui/react/text';

export function FaqSection() {
  return (
    <Column gap="l">
      <Text variant="heading" as="h2">Frequently Asked Questions</Text>
      <Accordion.Root>
        <Accordion.Item id="what-is">
          <Accordion.Header>
            <Accordion.Trigger>What is Tale UI?</Accordion.Trigger>
          </Accordion.Header>
          <Accordion.Panel>
            <Text>Tale UI is a design system providing accessible, themeable components.</Text>
          </Accordion.Panel>
        </Accordion.Item>
        <Accordion.Item id="install">
          <Accordion.Header>
            <Accordion.Trigger>How do I install it?</Accordion.Trigger>
          </Accordion.Header>
          <Accordion.Panel>
            <Text>Run <Text variant="mono">pnpm install @tale-ui/react</Text> to add Tale UI to your project.</Text>
          </Accordion.Panel>
        </Accordion.Item>
        <Accordion.Item id="accessible">
          <Accordion.Header>
            <Accordion.Trigger>Is it accessible?</Accordion.Trigger>
          </Accordion.Header>
          <Accordion.Panel>
            <Text>Yes. Tale UI is built on a React Aria foundation, ensuring full keyboard and screen reader support.</Text>
          </Accordion.Panel>
        </Accordion.Item>
        <Accordion.Item id="theme">
          <Accordion.Header>
            <Accordion.Trigger>Can I customize the theme?</Accordion.Trigger>
          </Accordion.Header>
          <Accordion.Panel>
            <Text>Yes. Theming is done via CSS custom properties, making it easy to adapt to any brand.</Text>
          </Accordion.Panel>
        </Accordion.Item>
      </Accordion.Root>
      <Banner.Root variant="info">
        <Banner.Description>
          Still have questions? <Link href="/contact">Contact us</Link>
        </Banner.Description>
      </Banner.Root>
    </Column>
  );
}

generalConventions.summary: Size token suffixes (-s, -m, -l) are CSS names — component size props use 'sm'/'md'/'lg' without a dash, EXCEPT Text which uses 'xs'/'s'/'m'/'l'
generalConventions.detail: the dash in `--space-s` is CSS token notation; prop value strings never start with a dash. `Text` is the only component whose `size` prop uses single-letter spacing-token values instead of the doubled component-size names; passing `size="sm"` to `Text` causes `Type '"sm"' is not assignable to type 'Size | undefined'`.

generalConventions.summary: Max gap value for Row/Column is '2xl' — there is no '3xl' or '4xl' gap token
generalConventions.detail: using out-of-range gap values produces no visible spacing because the token is undefined.

generalConventions.summary: Do not add variant, size, or color props to native HTML elements
generalConventions.detail:
these props only exist on Tale UI components and are silently ignored on `
`, `

`, ``, etc.

generalConventions.summary: Use from @tale-ui/react/image instead of a bare
generalConventions.detail: `Image` applies radius, lazy loading, and correct CSS; use its `radius` prop (not `borderRadius`) and `width`/`height` (not `size`).

generalConventions.summary: There is no Heading component — use <Text variant="heading" as="h2"> instead of bare <h2>
generalConventions.detail: importing `{ Heading }` from any `@tale-ui/react/*` path causes "has no exported member" TypeScript errors; bare heading tags bypass Tale UI typography tokens.

generalConventions.summary:
Never use bare

or for text content — always use from @tale-ui/react/text
generalConventions.detail: bare paragraph and span tags bypass Tale UI typography tokens and cannot accept `color` or `variant` props.
antiPatterns: This action cannot be undone.

generalConventions.summary: Component names must be valid PascalCase identifiers with no spaces
generalConventions.detail: exported React component names must be a single valid JavaScript/TypeScript identifier; spaces, hyphens, or other invalid characters in the function name cause parser errors before JSX is checked.

entries.Notes: Add full BEM or use `<IconButton>` pattern
Wraps any child element; typically plain text or an element
